theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Technology (+1.12%) and Energy (+0.43%) led the market today, while Materials (-2.52%) and Consumer Discretionary (-1.67%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged -0.55%, defensives -0.53%, and value/cyclicals -1.19% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 -0.43%, Dow -1.16%, Nasdaq -0.17% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -1.15%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX rose 4.84% to 16.91, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 1.53% to $4,070.30, crude oil was little changed at $74.47, and bitcoin slipped 2.06% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
